provisions.title: Short title and commencement
provisions.content:
1. Short title and commencement
This Act may be cited as the Mental Health Act, 2023, and shall come into operation on such a date as the Minister may, by Order published in the Gazette, appoint.

provisions.title: Interpretation

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Mental Health Board
provisions.title: Establishment of Board
provisions.content:
3. Establishment of Board
There is hereby established a Board, to be known as the Mental Health Board.

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Mental Health Board
provisions.title: Functions of Board

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Mental Health Board
provisions.title: Membership of Board

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Mental Health Board
provisions.title: Tenure of office
provisions.content:
6. Tenure of office
A member shall hold office for a period not exceeding three years and shall be eligible for reappointment for a further period not exceeding three years.

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Mental Health Board
provisions.title: Disqualification, suspension and removal of member

definitions.definition: an advance directive made under section 20
the Mental Health Board established under section 3
a person who resides with a person with mental illness and is responsible for providing care to that person, and includes a relative or any other person who performs this function
the Director of Social Protection from the Ministry responsible for local government

definitions.term: head of a mental health facility
definitions.definition: the accountable officer of a mental health facility
a condition of arrested or incomplete development of the mind

providing a person with treatment or a setting for treatment which—
( a ) meets the person’s treatment needs
a specified period granted by the head of a mental health facility, authorising a person receiving treatment to leave a mental health facility
biological and psychological treatments, social care for mental illness and curative and rehabilitative services provided either in a health or mental health facility or in a community
designated psychiatric facilities or general health facilities where mental health care and treatment is provided
a health professional registered under the Botswana Health Professions Act or the Nurses and Midwives Act and who has been trained to provide mental health care, treatment and rehabilitation services
an illness as determined under section 18, but does not include intellectual disability in itself
a person appointed under sections 27 and 28
assigned to it under the Prisons Act
the Mental Health Tribunal established under section 51.
